behavior_summary: U8P_GHOULEGG::leaveFastArea: When the relevant actor/item leaves a fast-area trigger, the script checks the egg's stored ID (Egg::getEggId). If that ID is >= 0x64 the script subtracts 0x64 and writes the new value back with Egg::setEggId — effectively removing an area-applied offset/flag from the egg state.

U8P_GHOULEGG::hatch: Implements the item's hatching behavior. It first ignores hatching if the egg ID is already >= 0x64. If the egg ID equals 0x01 it performs a U8P_GHOULEGG::hatch sequence: set egg ID to 0x64, create an NPC instance (Npc::create), set a status flag (Item::orStatus), place the NPC at the egg's coordinates (Item::pop using Item::getZ/Y/X), start/spawn the NPC's behavior (spawn -> METHOD::func15E7 with a small urandom-derived argument), then set the NPC's target, alignment, and in-combat state (Npc::setTarget, Npc::setAlignment, Npc::setInCombat). If egg ID != 0x01 there is an alternate probabilistic branch using urandom whose exact action is unclear from remnants.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GHOULEGG::leaveFastArea: condition Egg::getEggId(this) >= 0x64 gates a single write Egg::setEggId(get - 0x64). This is an area-exit guard that only mutates eggs with the high-bit/offset present.
U8P_GHOULEGG::hatch: top-level guard Egg::getEggId(this) >= 0x64 short-circuits (no-op) to prevent duplicate hatching or re-hatching altered eggs.

U8P_GHOULEGG::hatch: branching on Egg::getEggId == 0x01 triggers the full spawn/create sequence; else a probabilistic branch driven by urandom decides an alternate/rare behavior (remnant evidence only).
open_questions: What is the semantic meaning of the numeric value 0x64 when stored in the egg ID? (appears to be a marker/offset for a hatched or area-modified state; confidence: medium)

What exactly happens in the probabilistic branch inside U8P_GHOULEGG::hatch when Egg::getEggId(this) != 0x01? The remnants show an urandom check but the action is not present in the extracted nodes (confidence: low).
Are the constants passed to Npc::create (0x08h, 0x008Eh) selecting a specific NPC prototype and type? This is inferred from typical usage but not confirmed (confidence: medium).
Does subtracting 0x64 in U8P_GHOULEGG::leaveFastArea solely 'clear' a fast-area flag, or does it restore a previous ID namespace mapping used elsewhere? (confidence: low)

main_function.name: U8P_GHOULEGG::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: On leaving the fast-area trigger, detect eggs that carry an area-applied offset/flag (eggId >= 0x64) and remove that offset by subtracting 0x64 from the stored egg ID.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine when the actor/item leaves the fast-area trigger region (U8P_GHOULEGG::leaveFastArea event).
Runtime condition: only acts when Egg::getEggId(this) >= 0x64.

function_map.name: U8P_GHOULEGG::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: area-exit guard that normalizes egg state by removing an area-applied ID offset

function_map.name: U8P_GHOULEGG::hatch
function_map.role: handle egg hatching: create NPCs, set up status/placement, and start NPC behavior; contains probabilistic alternate branch

function_map.name: Npc::create
function_map.role: allocate/create the hatched NPC instance (returns pointer/handle)

function_map.name: Item::orStatus
function_map.role: apply status bits to created item/NPC

function_map.name: Item::pop
function_map.role: place/spawn created NPC at egg coordinates

function_map.name: spawn -> METHOD::func15E7
function_map.role: start a behavior/process on the spawned NPC

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: generate small random values used for probabilistic branching and spawn parameters

function_map.name: Npc::setTarget
function_map.role: set the created NPC's target (e.g., player/avatar)

function_map.name: Npc::setInCombat
function_map.role: mark the created NPC as 'in combat' to start combat behavior

external_calls.purpose: Read the egg's ID/state to determine whether to subtract 0x64.
Write back eggId - 0x64 to remove the fast-area offset/flag.
Check current egg state for branch selection (>=0x64 guard; ==0x01 U8P_GHOULEGG::hatch trigger).
Set egg ID to 0x64 as part of the U8P_GHOULEGG::hatch sequence (marking or preventing re-U8P_GHOULEGG::hatch).
Create the hatched NPC instance used for subsequent placement and behavior startup.
Apply a status flag (observed OR with 0x0080) to the created NPC/item after creation.
Place the created NPC at the location of the egg using Item::getZ/Y/X(this).
Launch the NPC's AI/behavior process with a randomized parameter derived from urandom.
Generate random values for a probabilistic branch and for the spawned process arguments.
Set the created NPC's initial target (argument observed as 0x01, likely the player/avatar).
Set the created NPC's alignment (observed constant 0x40).
Mark the hatched NPC as in-combat so it begins combat behavior immediately.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_GHOULEGG::leaveFastArea: When the relevant actor/item leaves a fast-area trigger, the script checks the egg's stored ID (Egg::getEggId). If that ID is >= 0x64 the script subtracts 0x64 and writes the new value back with Egg::setEggId — effectively removing an area-applied offset/flag from the egg state.
